questions.text: What mass extinction immediately preceded the rise of dinosaurs in the Triassic?
questions.explanation: The Permian-Triassic extinction ('The Great Dying') killed over 90% of marine species and preceded the Triassic, the period when dinosaurs first evolved.

questions.text: Which sauropod was distinctive for having longer front legs than hind legs?
questions.explanation: Brachiosaurus had longer front legs than hind legs, giving it an upward-sloping posture ideal for high browsing — unlike most other sauropods.

questions.text: What major botanical innovation occurred during the Cretaceous that transformed terrestrial ecosystems?
questions.explanation: Flowering plants (angiosperms) appeared and diversified explosively during the Cretaceous, fundamentally transforming terrestrial ecosystems and driving diversification of herbivorous dinosaurs.

questions.text: The Western Interior Seaway split which continent during the Late Cretaceous?
questions.explanation: The Western Interior Seaway was a vast inland sea stretching from the Gulf of Mexico to the Arctic Ocean, splitting North America into eastern and western landmasses from ~100-70 Mya.

choices: A supercontinent comprising all major landmasses
The name of the first land animal
questions.explanation: Pangaea was the supercontinent comprising all of Earth's major landmasses during the late Paleozoic and early Mesozoic. Its breakup during the Mesozoic isolated dinosaur populations.

questions.text: Pterosaurs were flying dinosaurs. True or false?
questions.explanation: False! Pterosaurs were flying reptiles closely related to dinosaurs but NOT dinosaurs themselves. They were a separate group of archosaurs that independently evolved powered flight.

questions.text: Why were Mesozoic atmospheric CO₂ levels much higher than today?
choices: Intense volcanic activity and continental breakup
questions.explanation: High Mesozoic CO₂ was driven by intense volcanic activity at mid-ocean ridges during continental breakup, large igneous province eruptions, and the absence of major carbon-sequestering ice sheets.

questions.text: What extinction event cleared the way for dinosaur dominance at the start of the Jurassic?
questions.explanation: The end-Triassic extinction (~201 Mya) eliminated many competing groups including rauisuchians and large amphibians, allowing dinosaurs to diversify and dominate from the Jurassic onward.

questions.text: Which group of predators filled the apex predator role on SOUTHERN continents during the Cretaceous?
questions.explanation: Abelisaurids (like Carnotaurus) were the dominant apex predators on southern continents (Gondwana), while tyrannosaurs dominated in the north (Laurasia).

questions.text: What remarkable discovery about Cretaceous mammals shows they weren't always tiny and submissive?
choices: Repenomamus was found with a baby dinosaur in its stomach
questions.explanation: Repenomamus, a badger-sized Cretaceous mammal, was found with a baby dinosaur in its stomach — proving that not all Mesozoic mammals were tiny prey items.
